sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 62

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 62:1
items.html:
(to the question) The vessel that raises MAN
Since the AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] of each degree fell to the degree below it, the GE of the lower one and the AHP of the upper one are found always cleaved to one another, both during the Katnut of the upper one and during the Gadlut [adulthood] of the upper one. For when the upper one raises its AHP, the GE of the lower one necessarily rise along with it. Therefore the AHP of each degree are discerned as the vessel that raises the MAN of the lower one — that is, its GE — since they are always cleaved to it. Only at the time of the lower one's Gadlut — that is, when it itself attains its own AHP and it, too, is completed with ten Sefirot — does it then part by itself, in its own degree, into a Partzuf distinct from the upper one. (Items 13 and 21)
